TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: ka sitsuayan neka pana, aya tjautsikel.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan nəka pana, aja cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: ka sicuayan neka pana, aya tjaucikel.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan nəka pana, aja ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there were no rivers, according to the story.

S.FORM: sa izua sisiɫ tjakanga qaḍis ritaɫ.
S.PHON: sa izua sisiʎ cakaŋa qaɖis ritaʎ.
S.FORM: sa izua sisilj tjakanga qadris ritalj.
S.PHON: sa izua sisiʎ ʦakaŋa qaɖis Ritaʎ.
S.TRANSL: The quaker-thrush, the kite, the hawk and the eagle were together,

S.FORM: qau maɫavar tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau maʎavar tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: qau maljavar tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau maʎavaR tiamaɉu.

S.FORM: "san pana-i; neka nu zaɫum a tja tekelen," aya azua sisiɫ katua tjakanga.
S.PHON: "san pana-i; nəka nu zaʎum a ca təkəɭən," aja azua sisiʎ katua cakaŋa.
S.FORM: "san pana-i; neka nu zaljum a tja tekelen," aya azua sisilj katua tjakanga.
S.PHON: "san pana-i; nəka nu zaʎum a ʦa təkəɣən," aja azua sisiʎ katua ʦakaŋa.
S.TRANSL: “Let’s make a river; we’ve no water to drink,” said the quaker-thrush and the kite.

S.FORM: manu inika ui a pusaladj azazua qaḍis katua ritaɫ.
S.PHON: manu inika ui a pusaɭaɉ azazua qaɖis katua ritaʎ.
S.FORM: manu inika ui a pusaladj azazua qadris katua ritalj.
S.PHON: manu inika ui a pusaɣaɉ azazua qaɖis katua Ritaʎ.
S.TRANSL: But the hawk and the eagle didn’t agree to help them.

S.FORM: "makapalak nia latip."
S.PHON: "makapaɭak nia ɭatip."
S.FORM: "makapalak nia latip."
S.PHON: "makapaɣak nia ɣatip."
S.TRANSL: Our wire braclets (? OA) are / will get damaged.”

S.FORM: qau seman pana azua sisiɫ katua tjakanga.
S.PHON: qau səman pana azua sisiʎ katua cakaŋa.
S.FORM: qau seman pana azua sisilj katua tjakanga.
S.PHON: qau səman pana azua sisiʎ katua ʦakaŋa.
S.TRANSL: So the thrush and the kite made a river.

S.FORM: "nu ini nu pusaladj, mayanga temekel tua zaɫum itjua nia sinan pana.
S.PHON: "nu ini nu pusaɭaɉ, majaŋa təməkəɭ tua zaʎum icua nia sinan pana.
S.FORM: "nu ini nu pusaladj, mayanga temekel tua zaljum itjua nia sinan pana.
S.PHON: "nu ini nu pusaɣaɉ, majaŋa təməkəɣ tua zaʎum iʦua nia sinan pana.
S.TRANSL: “If you don’t help, don’t drink from the river we’ve made.

S.FORM: nu temekel mun ki mun a matsangavu," aya azua tjakanga katua sisiɫ.
S.PHON: nu təməkəɭ mun ki mun a maʦaŋavu," aja azua cakaŋa katua sisiʎ.
S.FORM: nu temekel mun ki mun a macangavu," aya azua tjakanga katua sisilj.
S.PHON: nu təməkəɣ mun ki mun a maʦaŋavu," aja azua ʦakaŋa katua sisiʎ.
S.TRANSL: If you drink you will swell up,” said the thrush and the kite.

S.FORM: saka nu temekel azazua qaḍis katua ritaɫ, matsangavu, ini anga ka minlayap, aya tjautsikel.
S.PHON: saka nu təməkəɭ azazua qaɖis katua ritaʎ, maʦaŋavu, ini aŋa ka minɭajap, aja cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: saka nu temekel azazua qadris katua ritalj, macangavu, ini anga ka minlayap, aya tjaucikel.
S.PHON: saka nu təməkəɣ azazua qaɖis katua Ritaʎ, maʦaŋavu, ini aŋa ka minɣajap, aja ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: And when the hawk and eagle drank, they swelled up and couldn’t fly, according to the story.

S.FORM: sa nuka mavan tutsu nu temekel a qaḍis katua ritaɫ matsangavu angata.
S.PHON: sa nuka mavan tuʦu nu təməkəɭ a qaɖis katua ritaʎ maʦaŋavu aŋata.
S.FORM: sa nuka mavan tucu nu temekel a qadris katua ritalj macangavu angata.
S.PHON: sa nuka mavan tuʦu nu təməkəɣ a qaɖis katua Ritaʎ maʦaŋavu aŋata.
S.TRANSL: And even up to now if the hawk or the eagle drink they really do swell up.
